payload.answer: Rejecting the proposed 500-card atomized memory load; preserve as a single meta-level constraint rather than storing row-level facts.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable entity or stable fact should be committed from the provided context; the request is about filtering a proposed memory load, not substantive user content.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether to commit a large table atomized into 500 separate memory cards.

memory_cards.statement: Do not store one memory card per CSV row when the content is a large table to be atomized into hundreds of entries.

details: Chose not to commit 500 granular memory cards.
Stored only a minimal meta-level memory about avoiding this pattern.

repair_options: Summarize the table into a small number of durable higher-level memories.
Store only stable, user-relevant aggregates or exceptions, not every row.
Wait for explicit user intent before committing any row-level details.
